extra.reasoning:
The property (monotonicity on sweating: setting sweating=true never lowers acuity) is VIOLATED.

sweating appears in exactly one rule, r4 (priority 100), which requires all ten conjuncts (syncope, abdominal_pain, focal_weakness, stiff_neck, dizziness, palpitations, dyspnea, pallor, rash, sweating) and sets acuity := self_care (the lowest acuity). No other rule mentions sweating, and r4's priority (100) dominates every other rule (max other priority is 3).

Counterexample: set syncope, abdominal_pain, focal_weakness, stiff_neck, dizziness, palpitations, dyspnea, pallor, rash all true (vomiting false).

- With sweating=false: r4 does not fire. Matching rules include r3/r5/r6/r11 (priority 3 -> emergent) and several priority-2 rules (urgent). Highest priority among matches is 3, so acuity = emergent (highest acuity).
- With sweating=true (only sweating changed): r4 now fires with priority 100, overriding all priority-3 rules, so acuity := self_care (lowest acuity).

Toggling sweating from false to true drops acuity from emergent to self_care, which lowers acuity and violates the monotonicity property. The witness_assignment is the sweating=true input that produces self_care while its sweating=false counterpart produces emergent.
